steps.title: Identify the altitude-to-hypotenuse configuration
steps.content: Triangle $ABC$ has a right angle at $B$, and $BD$ is drawn from $B$ perpendicular to hypotenuse $AC$, with $D$ on $AC$. This is the classic right-triangle-with-altitude setup, with $AD = 8$ and $DC$ unknown.

steps.title: Apply the geometric mean altitude theorem
steps.content: The altitude to the hypotenuse is the geometric mean of the two hypotenuse segments:
